core/src/platform/android/AndroidMain.cpp
```
1 | #include <android_native_app_glue.h>
2 | #include <android/log.h>
3 | #include <android/input.h>
4 | #include <android/native_window.h>
5 | #include <exception>
6 | #include <SecretEngine/Core.h>
7 | #include <SecretEngine/ICore.h>
8 | #include <SecretEngine/ILogger.h>
9 | #include <SecretEngine/IRenderer.h>
10 | 
11 | #define LOG_TAG "SecretEngine_Native"
12 | #define LOGE(...) __android_log_print(ANDROID_LOG_ERROR, LOG_TAG, __VA_ARGS__)
13 | #define LOGI(...) __android_log_print(ANDROID_LOG_INFO, LOG_TAG, __VA_ARGS__)
14 | 
15 | // Include the plugin header directly in platform code for convenience
16 | #include "../../../../plugins/AndroidInput/src/InputPlugin.h"
17 | 
18 | static int32_t handle_input(struct android_app* app, AInputEvent* event) {
19 |     if (app->userData == nullptr) return 0;
20 |     SecretEngine::ICore* core = static_cast<SecretEngine::ICore*>(app->userData);
21 |     
22 |     if (AInputEvent_getType(event) == AINPUT_EVENT_TYPE_MOTION) {
23 |         int32_t action = AMotionEvent_getAction(event);
24 |         int32_t actionCode = action & AMOTION_EVENT_ACTION_MASK;
25 |         
26 |         auto inputPlugin = core->GetCapability("input");
27 |         if (!inputPlugin) return 1;
28 |         auto input = static_cast<SecretEngine::AndroidInput*>(inputPlugin);
29 | 
30 |         // Get screen dimensions
31 |         float screenWidth = 1080.0f;
32 |         float screenHeight = 2400.0f;
33 |         if (app->window) {
34 |             screenWidth = (float)ANativeWindow_getWidth(app->window);
35 |             screenHeight = (float)ANativeWindow_getHeight(app->window);
36 |         }
37 | 
38 |         if (actionCode == AMOTION_EVENT_ACTION_MOVE) {
39 |             size_t pointerCount = AMotionEvent_getPointerCount(event);
40 |             for (size_t i = 0; i < pointerCount; i++) {
41 |                 float x = AMotionEvent_getX(event, i);
42 |                 float y = AMotionEvent_getY(event, i);
43 |                 int32_t pointerId = AMotionEvent_getPointerId(event, i);
44 |                 input->HandleTouch(action, x, y, pointerId, screenWidth, screenHeight);
45 |             }
46 |         } else {
47 |             int32_t pointerIndex = (action & AMOTION_EVENT_ACTION_POINTER_INDEX_MASK) >> AMOTION_EVENT_ACTION_POINTER_INDEX_SHIFT;
48 |             float x = AMotionEvent_getX(event, pointerIndex);
49 |             float y = AMotionEvent_getY(event, pointerIndex);
50 |             int32_t pointerId = AMotionEvent_getPointerId(event, pointerIndex);
51 |             input->HandleTouch(action, x, y, pointerId, screenWidth, screenHeight);
52 |         }
53 |         
54 |         return 1;
55 |     }
56 |     
57 |     return 0;
58 | }
59 | 
60 | static void handle_cmd(struct android_app* app, int32_t cmd) {
61 |     if (app->userData == nullptr) {
62 |         LOGE("handle_cmd: app->userData is nullptr!");
63 |         return;
64 |     }
65 |     SecretEngine::ICore* core = static_cast<SecretEngine::ICore*>(app->userData);
66 | 
67 |     switch (cmd) {
68 |         case APP_CMD_INIT_WINDOW:
69 |             if (app->window != nullptr) {
70 |                 LOGI("APP_CMD_INIT_WINDOW: Window is ready");
71 |                 if(core->GetLogger()) {
72 |                     core->GetLogger()->LogInfo("AndroidMain", "Window Ready - Initializing Graphics...");
73 |                 }
74 |                 
75 |                 auto plugin = core->GetCapability("rendering");
76 |                 if (plugin) {
77 |                     LOGI("Renderer plugin found, calling InitializeHardware...");
78 |                     SecretEngine::IRenderer* renderer = static_cast<SecretEngine::IRenderer*>(plugin);
79 |                     try {
80 |                         renderer->InitializeHardware(app->window);
81 |                         LOGI("InitializeHardware completed");
82 |                     } catch (...) {
83 |                         LOGE("CRITICAL: Exception during renderer->InitializeHardware()");
84 |                         if(core->GetLogger()) {
85 |                             core->GetLogger()->LogError("AndroidMain", "Renderer initialization failed - continuing without graphics");
86 |                         }
87 |                     }
88 |                 } else {
89 |                     LOGE("WARNING: No rendering plugin found!");
90 |                     if(core->GetLogger()) {
91 |                         core->GetLogger()->LogWarning("AndroidMain", "No renderer available - running headless");
92 |                     }
93 |                 }
94 |             } else {
95 |                 LOGE("APP_CMD_INIT_WINDOW received but app->window is nullptr!");
96 |             }
97 |             break;
98 |         case APP_CMD_TERM_WINDOW:
99 |             LOGI("APP_CMD_TERM_WINDOW: Window being terminated");
100 |             if(core->GetLogger()) {
101 |                 core->GetLogger()->LogInfo("AndroidMain", "Window Terminated");
102 |             }
103 |             break;
104 |         case APP_CMD_GAINED_FOCUS:
105 |             LOGI("APP_CMD_GAINED_FOCUS: App gained focus");
106 |             break;
107 |         case APP_CMD_LOST_FOCUS:
108 |             LOGI("APP_CMD_LOST_FOCUS: App lost focus");
109 |             break;
110 |         case APP_CMD_START:
111 |             LOGI("APP_CMD_START: App started");
112 |             break;
113 |         case APP_CMD_RESUME:
114 |             LOGI("APP_CMD_RESUME: App resumed");
115 |             break;
116 |         case APP_CMD_PAUSE:
117 |             LOGI("APP_CMD_PAUSE: App paused");
118 |             break;
119 |         case APP_CMD_STOP:
120 |             LOGI("APP_CMD_STOP: App stopped");
121 |             break;
122 |     }
123 | }
124 | 
125 | extern "C" {
126 |     // Global android_app pointer for shader loading
127 |     struct android_app* g_AndroidApp = nullptr;
128 |     
129 |     void android_main(struct android_app* state) {
130 |         LOGI("========================================");
131 |         LOGI("SecretEngine android_main() ENTRY POINT");
132 |         LOGI("========================================");
133 | 
134 |         // 1. Safety check for state
135 |         if (state == nullptr) {
136 |             LOGE("FATAL: android_app state is nullptr!");
137 |             return;
138 |         }
139 |         LOGI("✓ android_app state is valid");
140 |         
141 |         // Store global reference for shader loading
142 |         g_AndroidApp = state;
143 | 
144 |         // 2. Get Engine Core
145 |         SecretEngine::ICore* core = SecretEngine::GetEngineCore();
146 |         if (core == nullptr) {
147 |             LOGE("FATAL: SecretEngine::GetEngineCore() returned nullptr!");
148 |             return;
149 |         }
150 |         LOGI("✓ Engine Core obtained successfully");
151 | 
152 |         state->userData = core;
153 |         state->onAppCmd = handle_cmd;
154 |         state->onInputEvent = handle_input; // Register touch input
155 |         
156 |         LOGI("✓ Event handlers registered");
157 | 
158 |         // 3. Initialize Core with Error Checking
159 |         bool coreInitialized = false;
160 |         try {
161 |             LOGI("Initializing Engine Core...");
162 |             core->Initialize();
163 |             coreInitialized = true;
164 |             LOGI("✓ Core Initialized successfully");
165 |             if (core->GetLogger()) {
166 |                 core->GetLogger()->LogInfo("AndroidMain", "Core Initialized - App is running");
167 |             }
168 |         } catch (const std::exception& e) {
169 |             LOGE("EXCEPTION during core->Initialize(): %s", e.what());
170 |         } catch (...) {
171 |             LOGE("UNKNOWN EXCEPTION during core->Initialize()");
172 |         }
173 | 
174 |         if (!coreInitialized) {
175 |             LOGE("FATAL: Core initialization failed - cannot continue");
176 |             return;
177 |         }
178 | 
179 |         // 4. PERSISTENT EVENT LOOP
180 |         LOGI("========================================");
181 |         LOGI("Entering Main Event Loop");
182 |         LOGI("App should now be visible and running");
183 |         LOGI("========================================");
184 |         
185 |         int frameCount = 0;
186 |         while (true) {
187 |             int events;
188 |             struct android_poll_source* source;
189 | 
190 |             // Use -1 (wait) if no window to save battery, 0 to poll when active
191 |             int timeout = (state->window != nullptr) ? 0 : -1;
192 | 
193 |             // Process all pending events (non-blocking when window is available)
194 |             while ((ALooper_pollAll(timeout, nullptr, &events, (void**)&source)) >= 0) {
195 |                 if (source != nullptr) {
196 |                     source->process(state, source);
197 |                 }
198 | 
199 |                 // Check for destroy request
200 |                 if (state->destroyRequested != 0) {
201 |                     LOGI("========================================");
202 |                     LOGI("Destroy requested - shutting down cleanly");
203 |                     LOGI("========================================");
204 |                     if (core->GetLogger()) {
205 |                         core->GetLogger()->LogInfo("AndroidMain", "App closing normally");
206 |                     }
207 |                     core->Shutdown();
208 |                     LOGI("✓ Shutdown complete");
209 |                     return;
210 |                 }
211 |                 
212 |                 // After processing one event, break to render a frame
213 |                 // This ensures we don't get stuck processing events
214 |                 if (state->window != nullptr) {
215 |                     break;
216 |                 }
217 |             }
218 | 
219 |             // Update and render every frame when window is available
220 |             if (state->window != nullptr) {
221 |                 try {
222 |                     core->Update();
223 |                     frameCount++;
224 |                     
225 |                     // Log every 300 frames (~5 seconds at 60fps) to confirm app is alive
226 |                     if (frameCount % 300 == 0) {
227 |                         LOGI("App running: %d frames rendered", frameCount);
228 |                     }
229 |                 } catch (const std::exception& e) {
230 |                     LOGE("Exception in core->Update(): %s", e.what());
231 |                 } catch (...) {
232 |                     LOGE("Unknown exception in core->Update()");
233 |                 }
234 |             }
235 |         }
236 |     }
237 | }
```

docs/reference/texture/more/IMPLEMENTATION_COMPARISON.md
```
1 | # BINDLESS TEXTURE IMPLEMENTATION COMPARISON
2 | 
3 | ## Key Differences Between Approaches
4 | 
5 | ### ✅ WHAT THE DOCUMENT GETS RIGHT (That Your Implementation Should Add)
6 | 
7 | #### 1. **VulkanDevice.cpp - Descriptor Indexing Features**
8 | 
9 | **CRITICAL ADDITION NEEDED:**
10 | 
11 | ```cpp
12 | // In CreateLogicalDevice()
13 | 
14 | // Query support first
15 | VkPhysicalDeviceDescriptorIndexingFeatures indexingFeatures = {
16 |     VK_STRUCTURE_TYPE_PHYSICAL_DEVICE_DESCRIPTOR_INDEXING_FEATURES
17 | };
18 | 
19 | VkPhysicalDeviceFeatures2 supportedFeatures = {
20 |     VK_STRUCTURE_TYPE_PHYSICAL_DEVICE_FEATURES_2
21 | };
22 | supportedFeatures.pNext = &indexingFeatures;
23 | 
24 | vkGetPhysicalDeviceFeatures2(m_physicalDevice, &supportedFeatures);
25 | 
26 | // Then enable required features
27 | VkPhysicalDeviceDescriptorIndexingFeatures enabledIndexingFeatures = {
28 |     VK_STRUCTURE_TYPE_PHYSICAL_DEVICE_DESCRIPTOR_INDEXING_FEATURES
29 | };
30 | enabledIndexingFeatures.shaderSampledImageArrayNonUniformIndexing = VK_TRUE;
31 | enabledIndexingFeatures.runtimeDescriptorArray = VK_TRUE;
32 | enabledIndexingFeatures.descriptorBindingPartiallyBound = VK_TRUE;
33 | enabledIndexingFeatures.descriptorBindingVariableDescriptorCount = VK_TRUE;
34 | 
35 | // Chain to device creation
36 | VkPhysicalDeviceFeatures2 deviceFeatures2 = {
37 |     VK_STRUCTURE_TYPE_PHYSICAL_DEVICE_FEATURES_2
38 | };
39 | deviceFeatures2.features.samplerAnisotropy = VK_TRUE;
40 | deviceFeatures2.pNext = &enabledIndexingFeatures;
41 | 
42 | VkDeviceCreateInfo createInfo = {VK_STRUCTURE_TYPE_DEVICE_CREATE_INFO};
43 | createInfo.pNext = &deviceFeatures2;  // ← CHAIN THE FEATURES
44 | 
45 | // Also add extension
46 | const char* deviceExtensions[] = { 
47 |     VK_KHR_SWAPCHAIN_EXTENSION_NAME,
48 |     VK_EXT_DESCRIPTOR_INDEXING_EXTENSION_NAME  // ← ADD THIS
49 | };
50 | ```
51 | 
52 | **WHY THIS MATTERS:**
53 | - Without these features enabled, `nonuniformEXT()` in shaders will fail
54 | - The GPU won't allow partially bound descriptor arrays
55 | - Your app will crash or have validation errors
56 | 
57 | ---
58 | 
59 | #### 2. **Descriptor Set Layout Flags**
60 | 
61 | **CRITICAL FLAGS NEEDED:**
62 | 
63 | ```cpp
64 | // In CreateDescriptorSet()
65 | 
66 | VkDescriptorBindingFlags bindFlags[] = {
67 |     0,  // Binding 0 (SSBO) - standard
68 |     
69 |     // Binding 1 (Textures) - MUST HAVE THESE FLAGS
70 |     VK_DESCRIPTOR_BINDING_PARTIALLY_BOUND_BIT_EXT |           // ← Allows empty slots
71 |     VK_DESCRIPTOR_BINDING_UPDATE_AFTER_BIND_BIT_EXT |         // ← Update while rendering
72 |     VK_DESCRIPTOR_BINDING_UPDATE_UNUSED_WHILE_PENDING_BIT_EXT // ← Update anytime
73 | };
74 | 
75 | VkDescriptorSetLayoutBindingFlagsCreateInfo flagsInfo = {
76 |     VK_STRUCTURE_TYPE_DESCRIPTOR_SET_LAYOUT_BINDING_FLAGS_CREATE_INFO
77 | };
78 | flagsInfo.bindingCount = 2;
79 | flagsInfo.pBindingFlags = bindFlags;
80 | 
81 | VkDescriptorSetLayoutCreateInfo layoutInfo = {
82 |     VK_STRUCTURE_TYPE_DESCRIPTOR_SET_LAYOUT_CREATE_INFO
83 | };
84 | layoutInfo.pNext = &flagsInfo;  // ← CHAIN THE FLAGS
85 | layoutInfo.flags = VK_DESCRIPTOR_SET_LAYOUT_CREATE_UPDATE_AFTER_BIND_POOL_BIT_EXT;  // ← REQUIRED
86 | ```
87 | 
88 | **WHY THIS MATTERS:**
89 | - Without `PARTIALLY_BOUND`: Must fill ALL 1024 texture slots or crash
90 | - Without `UPDATE_AFTER_BIND`: Can't load textures while rendering
91 | - Without pool flag: Descriptor allocation will fail
92 | 
93 | ---
94 | 
95 | #### 3. **Shader Extensions**
96 | 
97 | **REQUIRED IN FRAGMENT SHADER:**
98 | 
99 | ```glsl
100 | #version 450
101 | #extension GL_EXT_nonuniform_qualifier : enable  // ← ABSOLUTELY REQUIRED
102 | 
103 | layout(binding = 1) uniform sampler2D globalTextures[1024];
104 | 
105 | void main() {
106 |     // MUST use nonuniformEXT() wrapper
107 |     vec4 color = texture(globalTextures[nonuniformEXT(fragTexID)], fragUV);
108 |     //                                  ↑↑↑↑↑↑↑↑↑↑↑↑↑↑
109 |     //                           This tells GPU: "index varies per pixel"
110 | }
111 | ```
112 | 
113 | **WHY THIS MATTERS:**
114 | - Without `nonuniformEXT()`: Undefined behavior (wrong textures, crashes)
115 | - GPU assumes all pixels in a warp use same texture index
116 | - Most GPUs will silently render incorrectly
117 | 
118 | ---
119 | 
120 | ### ✅ WHAT YOUR IMPLEMENTATION DOES BETTER
121 | 
122 | #### 1. **TextureManager Architecture**
123 | 
124 | **YOUR ADVANTAGE:**
125 | - Centralized texture management class
126 | - ASTC compression built-in
127 | - Material system support
128 | - Texture atlas support
129 | - Better encapsulation
130 | 
131 | **DOCUMENT'S APPROACH:**
132 | - Simple `Texture` class (less features)
133 | - No compression
134 | - No material system
135 | 
136 | **VERDICT:** Your architecture is production-ready ✅
137 | 
138 | ---
139 | 
140 | #### 2. **ASTC Compression**
141 | 
142 | **YOUR ADVANTAGE:**
143 | - Automatic PNG → ASTC conversion
144 | - Runtime compression with `astcenc.h`
145 | - Format auto-detection (ASTC → BC7 → RGBA8)
146 | - Memory savings: 4-32x
147 | 
148 | **DOCUMENT'S APPROACH:**
149 | - Assumes pre-compressed `.astc` files
150 | - Manual conversion needed
151 | - No fallback system
152 | 
153 | **VERDICT:** Your system is far more practical ✅
154 | 
155 | ---
156 | 
157 | #### 3. **Staging Buffer Design**
158 | 
159 | **YOUR ADVANTAGE:**
160 | - Single persistent 64MB staging buffer
161 | - Reused across all uploads
162 | - No allocations per texture
163 | 
164 | **DOCUMENT'S APPROACH:**
165 | - Creates staging buffer per texture
166 | - More overhead
167 | 
168 | **VERDICT:** Your approach is more efficient ✅
169 | 
170 | ---
171 | 
172 | #### 4. **Double Buffering**
173 | 
174 | **YOUR ADVANTAGE:**
175 | - Both approaches use double buffering correctly
176 | - Both update both descriptor sets
177 | 
178 | **VERDICT:** Tie ✅
179 | 
180 | ---
181 | 
182 | ### 🔧 CRITICAL FIXES NEEDED IN YOUR CODE
183 | 
184 | #### Fix 1: Add Descriptor Indexing to VulkanDevice
185 | 
186 | **File:** `VulkanDevice.cpp`
187 | 
188 | ```cpp
189 | // Replace CreateLogicalDevice() with the updated version
190 | // that enables descriptor indexing features
191 | ```
192 | 
193 | See: `VulkanDevice_Updated.cpp`
194 | 
195 | ---
196 | 
197 | #### Fix 2: Add Binding Flags to Descriptor Layout
198 | 
199 | **File:** `MegaGeometryRenderer.cpp` (or your textured version)
200 | 
201 | ```cpp
202 | // In CreateDescriptorSet(), add the binding flags
203 | // as shown in MegaGeometryDescriptorSetup_Corrected.cpp
204 | ```
205 | 
206 | **Without this:** You'll get validation errors like:
207 | ```
208 | VUID-VkWriteDescriptorSet-dstArrayElement: Descriptor binding not partially bound
209 | ```
210 | 
211 | ---
212 | 
213 | #### Fix 3: Ensure Shader Uses nonuniformEXT
214 | 
215 | **File:** `mega_geometry_textured.frag`
216 | 
217 | Your shader already has this ✅:
218 | ```glsl
219 | #extension GL_EXT_nonuniform_qualifier : enable
220 | vec4 texColor = texture(textures[nonuniformEXT(fragTextureID)], fragTexCoord);
221 | ```
222 | 
223 | **This is correct!**
224 | 
225 | ---
226 | 
227 | ### 📊 IMPLEMENTATION COMPARISON TABLE
228 | 
229 | | Feature | Your Implementation | Document | Winner |
230 | |---------|-------------------|----------|--------|
231 | | **Descriptor Indexing Setup** | ❌ Missing | ✅ Correct | Document |
232 | | **Binding Flags** | ❌ Missing | ✅ Correct | Document |
233 | | **ASTC Compression** | ✅ Runtime | ❌ Pre-compressed only | You |
234 | | **Texture Manager** | ✅ Full-featured | ❌ Basic | You |
235 | | **Material System** | ✅ PBR support | ❌ None | You |
236 | | **Texture Atlas** | ✅ Built-in | ❌ None | You |
237 | | **Staging Buffer** | ✅ Efficient | ⚠️ Per-texture | You |
238 | | **Error Handling** | ✅ Comprehensive | ⚠️ Basic | You |
239 | | **Documentation** | ✅ Extensive | ⚠️ Minimal | You |
240 | 
241 | ---
242 | 
243 | ### 🎯 RECOMMENDED INTEGRATION STRATEGY
244 | 
245 | **Step 1:** Update `VulkanDevice.cpp`
246 | - Use the corrected version with descriptor indexing features
247 | - Add the extension to device creation
248 | 
249 | **Step 2:** Update `MegaGeometryRenderer::CreateDescriptorSet()`
250 | - Add binding flags (PARTIALLY_BOUND, UPDATE_AFTER_BIND)
251 | - Add pool creation flag (UPDATE_AFTER_BIND_POOL_BIT)
252 | 
253 | **Step 3:** Keep Everything Else From Your Implementation
254 | - TextureManager architecture ✅
255 | - ASTC compression ✅
256 | - Material system ✅
257 | - Shader code ✅
258 | 
259 | **Step 4:** Test on Real Hardware
260 | ```cpp
261 | // Query support at runtime
262 | VkPhysicalDeviceDescriptorIndexingFeatures features = {...};
263 | vkGetPhysicalDeviceFeatures2(gpu, &features);
264 | 
265 | if (!features.shaderSampledImageArrayNonUniformIndexing) {
266 |     LOG_ERROR("Bindless textures not supported on this GPU!");
267 |     // Fall back to traditional descriptor sets
268 | }
269 | ```
270 | 
271 | ---
272 | 
273 | ### 🚨 COMMON PITFALLS TO AVOID
274 | 
275 | #### Pitfall 1: Forgetting Chain Structures
276 | 
277 | **WRONG:**
278 | ```cpp
279 | VkDeviceCreateInfo createInfo = {VK_STRUCTURE_TYPE_DEVICE_CREATE_INFO};
280 | // Missing pNext chain!
281 | ```
282 | 
283 | **RIGHT:**
284 | ```cpp
285 | VkDeviceCreateInfo createInfo = {VK_STRUCTURE_TYPE_DEVICE_CREATE_INFO};
286 | createInfo.pNext = &deviceFeatures2;  // ← Chain the features
287 | ```
288 | 
289 | ---
290 | 
291 | #### Pitfall 2: Wrong Pool Flags
292 | 
293 | **WRONG:**
294 | ```cpp
295 | VkDescriptorPoolCreateInfo poolInfo = {...};
296 | // Missing UPDATE_AFTER_BIND_BIT
297 | ```
298 | 
299 | **RIGHT:**
300 | ```cpp
301 | VkDescriptorPoolCreateInfo poolInfo = {...};
302 | poolInfo.flags = VK_DESCRIPTOR_POOL_CREATE_UPDATE_AFTER_BIND_BIT_EXT;
303 | ```
304 | 
305 | ---
306 | 
307 | #### Pitfall 3: Missing Shader Extension
308 | 
309 | **WRONG:**
310 | ```glsl
311 | // No extension declared
312 | vec4 color = texture(textures[fragTexID], uv);  // ← Undefined behavior!
313 | ```
314 | 
315 | **RIGHT:**
316 | ```glsl
317 | #extension GL_EXT_nonuniform_qualifier : enable
318 | vec4 color = texture(textures[nonuniformEXT(fragTexID)], uv);  // ← Correct
319 | ```
320 | 
321 | ---
322 | 
323 | ### 📝 FINAL VERDICT
324 | 
325 | **Your implementation is 90% correct and far more feature-complete.**
326 | 
327 | You just need to add these 2 critical fixes:
328 | 
329 | 1. ✅ **Descriptor indexing features in VulkanDevice** (from document)
330 | 2. ✅ **Binding flags in descriptor layout** (from document)
331 | 
332 | Everything else in your implementation is superior:
333 | - Better architecture
334 | - More features
335 | - Better performance
336 | - Production-ready
337 | 
338 | ---
339 | 
340 | ### 🔗 FILES TO USE
341 | 
342 | **Use Your Files:**
343 | - ✅ `TextureManager.h/cpp`
344 | - ✅ `MegaGeometryRendererTextured.h`
345 | - ✅ `mega_geometry_textured.vert/frag`
346 | - ✅ `ASTCConverter.h`
347 | 
348 | **Apply Fixes From Document:**
349 | - ⚠️ Update `VulkanDevice.cpp` with descriptor indexing
350 | - ⚠️ Update `CreateDescriptorSet()` with binding flags
351 | 
352 | **Result:** Best of both worlds! 🎉
```

docs/reference/texture/more/MegaGeometryDescriptorSetup_Corrected.cpp
```
1 | // ============================================================================
2 | // DESCRIPTOR SET CREATION - CORRECTED FOR BINDLESS TEXTURES
3 | // This replaces the CreateDescriptorSet() function in MegaGeometryRenderer.cpp
4 | // ============================================================================
5 | 
6 | bool MegaGeometryRenderer::CreateDescriptorSet() {
7 |     // ============================================================================
8 |     // BINDING 0: Instance SSBO (Standard Storage Buffer)
9 |     // ============================================================================
10 |     VkDescriptorSetLayoutBinding ssboBind = {};
11 |     ssboBind.binding = 0;
12 |     ssboBind.descriptorType = VK_DESCRIPTOR_TYPE_STORAGE_BUFFER;
13 |     ssboBind.descriptorCount = 1;
14 |     ssboBind.stageFlags = VK_SHADER_STAGE_VERTEX_BIT | VK_SHADER_STAGE_COMPUTE_BIT;
15 |     ssboBind.pImmutableSamplers = nullptr;
16 |     
17 |     // ============================================================================
18 |     // BINDING 1: Bindless Texture Array (1024 or 16384 textures)
19 |     // ============================================================================
20 |     VkDescriptorSetLayoutBinding texBind = {};
21 |     texBind.binding = 1;
22 |     texBind.descriptorCount = MAX_TEXTURES;  // 1024 recommended, 16384 maximum
23 |     texBind.descriptorType = VK_DESCRIPTOR_TYPE_COMBINED_IMAGE_SAMPLER;
24 |     texBind.stageFlags = VK_SHADER_STAGE_FRAGMENT_BIT;
25 |     texBind.pImmutableSamplers = nullptr;
26 |     
27 |     VkDescriptorSetLayoutBinding bindings[] = { ssboBind, texBind };
28 |     
29 |     // ============================================================================
30 |     // CRITICAL: Binding Flags for Bindless Operation
31 |     // ============================================================================
32 |     VkDescriptorBindingFlags bindFlags[] = {
33 |         // Binding 0 (SSBO): Standard, no special flags
34 |         0,
35 |         
36 |         // Binding 1 (Textures): Enable bindless features
37 |         VK_DESCRIPTOR_BINDING_PARTIALLY_BOUND_BIT_EXT |           // Allow unused slots
38 |         VK_DESCRIPTOR_BINDING_UPDATE_AFTER_BIND_BIT_EXT |         // Update while in use
39 |         VK_DESCRIPTOR_BINDING_UPDATE_UNUSED_WHILE_PENDING_BIT_EXT // Update unused slots anytime
40 |     };
41 |     
42 |     VkDescriptorSetLayoutBindingFlagsCreateInfo flagsInfo = {
43 |         VK_STRUCTURE_TYPE_DESCRIPTOR_SET_LAYOUT_BINDING_FLAGS_CREATE_INFO
44 |     };
45 |     flagsInfo.bindingCount = 2;
46 |     flagsInfo.pBindingFlags = bindFlags;
47 |     
48 |     // ============================================================================
49 |     // DESCRIPTOR SET LAYOUT CREATION
50 |     // ============================================================================
51 |     VkDescriptorSetLayoutCreateInfo layoutInfo = {
52 |         VK_STRUCTURE_TYPE_DESCRIPTOR_SET_LAYOUT_CREATE_INFO
53 |     };
54 |     layoutInfo.pNext = &flagsInfo;  // Chain the binding flags
55 |     
56 |     // CRITICAL: Allow updates after binding (required for bindless)
57 |     layoutInfo.flags = VK_DESCRIPTOR_SET_LAYOUT_CREATE_UPDATE_AFTER_BIND_POOL_BIT_EXT;
58 |     
59 |     layoutInfo.bindingCount = 2;
60 |     layoutInfo.pBindings = bindings;
61 |     
62 |     if (vkCreateDescriptorSetLayout(m_vkDevice, &layoutInfo, nullptr, &m_descriptorSetLayout) != VK_SUCCESS) {
63 |         m_core->GetLogger()->LogError("MegaGeometry", "Failed to create descriptor set layout");
64 |         return false;
65 |     }
66 |     
67 |     // ============================================================================
68 |     // DESCRIPTOR POOL CREATION
69 |     // ============================================================================
70 |     VkDescriptorPoolSize poolSizes[] = {
71 |         {VK_DESCRIPTOR_TYPE_STORAGE_BUFFER, 2},  // 2 for double buffering
72 |         {VK_DESCRIPTOR_TYPE_COMBINED_IMAGE_SAMPLER, MAX_TEXTURES * 2}  // Double buffered
73 |     };
74 |     
75 |     VkDescriptorPoolCreateInfo poolInfo = {
76 |         VK_STRUCTURE_TYPE_DESCRIPTOR_POOL_CREATE_INFO
77 |     };
78 |     
79 |     // CRITICAL: Allow updates after binding
80 |     poolInfo.flags = VK_DESCRIPTOR_POOL_CREATE_UPDATE_AFTER_BIND_BIT_EXT;
81 |     
82 |     poolInfo.maxSets = 2;  // Double buffered
83 |     poolInfo.poolSizeCount = 2;
84 |     poolInfo.pPoolSizes = poolSizes;
85 |     
86 |     VkDescriptorPool pool;
87 |     if (vkCreateDescriptorPool(m_vkDevice, &poolInfo, nullptr, &pool) != VK_SUCCESS) {
88 |         m_core->GetLogger()->LogError("MegaGeometry", "Failed to create descriptor pool");
89 |         return false;
90 |     }
91 |     
92 |     // ============================================================================
93 |     // ALLOCATE DESCRIPTOR SETS (Double Buffered)
94 |     // ============================================================================
95 |     for (int i = 0; i < 2; ++i) {
96 |         VkDescriptorSetAllocateInfo allocInfo = {
97 |             VK_STRUCTURE_TYPE_DESCRIPTOR_SET_ALLOCATE_INFO
98 |         };
99 |         allocInfo.descriptorPool = pool;
100 |         allocInfo.descriptorSetCount = 1;
101 |         allocInfo.pSetLayouts = &m_descriptorSetLayout;
102 |         
103 |         if (vkAllocateDescriptorSets(m_vkDevice, &allocInfo, &m_descriptorSet[i]) != VK_SUCCESS) {
104 |             m_core->GetLogger()->LogError("MegaGeometry", "Failed to allocate descriptor sets");
105 |             return false;
106 |         }
107 |         
108 |         // ============================================================================
109 |         // WRITE SSBO BINDING (Binding 0)
110 |         // ============================================================================
111 |         VkDescriptorBufferInfo bufferInfo = {};
112 |         bufferInfo.buffer = m_instanceSSBO[i];
113 |         bufferInfo.offset = 0;
114 |         bufferInfo.range = VK_WHOLE_SIZE;
115 |         
116 |         VkWriteDescriptorSet write = {VK_STRUCTURE_TYPE_WRITE_DESCRIPTOR_SET};
117 |         write.dstSet = m_descriptorSet[i];
118 |         write.dstBinding = 0;
119 |         write.dstArrayElement = 0;
120 |         write.descriptorType = VK_DESCRIPTOR_TYPE_STORAGE_BUFFER;
121 |         write.descriptorCount = 1;
122 |         write.pBufferInfo = &bufferInfo;
123 |         
124 |         vkUpdateDescriptorSets(m_vkDevice, 1, &write, 0, nullptr);
125 |         
126 |         // Note: Texture bindings (Binding 1) are updated dynamically in LoadTexture()
127 |     }
128 |     
129 |     m_core->GetLogger()->LogInfo("MegaGeometry", "✓ Bindless descriptor sets created");
130 |     return true;
131 | }
132 | 
133 | // ============================================================================
134 | // TEXTURE LOADING - RUNTIME DESCRIPTOR UPDATE
135 | // ============================================================================
136 | 
137 | int32_t MegaGeometryRenderer::LoadTexture(const char* path) {
138 |     // 1. Check cache
139 |     if (m_texturePathToID.count(path)) {
140 |         return m_texturePathToID[path];
141 |     }
142 |     
143 |     // 2. Safety limit
144 |     if (m_loadedTextures.size() >= MAX_TEXTURES) {
145 |         m_core->GetLogger()->LogError("MegaGeometry", "Texture limit reached!");
146 |         return 0;  // Return slot 0 (default white texture)
147 |     }
148 |     
149 |     // 3. Load texture using TextureManager or direct Texture class
150 |     Texture* tex = new Texture(m_device);
151 |     
152 |     // Need command pool for upload - create one in Initialize() if needed
153 |     if (!tex->Load(path, m_transferCommandPool, m_device->GetGraphicsQueue())) {
154 |         m_core->GetLogger()->LogError("MegaGeometry", 
155 |             (std::string("Failed to load: ") + path).c_str());
156 |         delete tex;
157 |         return -1;
158 |     }
159 |     
160 |     // 4. Assign ID
161 |     uint32_t id = (uint32_t)m_loadedTextures.size();
162 |     m_loadedTextures.push_back(tex);
163 |     m_texturePathToID[path] = id;
164 |     
165 |     // 5. Update GPU descriptor immediately
166 |     UpdateTextureDescriptor(id, tex);
167 |     
168 |     m_core->GetLogger()->LogInfo("MegaGeometry", 
169 |         (std::string("✓ Loaded texture [") + std::to_string(id) + "]: " + path).c_str());
170 |     
171 |     return id;
172 | }
173 | 
174 | void MegaGeometryRenderer::UpdateTextureDescriptor(uint32_t slot, Texture* tex) {
175 |     VkDescriptorImageInfo imageInfo = {};
176 |     imageInfo.imageLayout = VK_IMAGE_LAYOUT_SHADER_READ_ONLY_OPTIMAL;
177 |     imageInfo.imageView = tex->GetImageView();
178 |     imageInfo.sampler = tex->GetSampler();
179 |     
180 |     VkWriteDescriptorSet write = {VK_STRUCTURE_TYPE_WRITE_DESCRIPTOR_SET};
181 |     write.dstBinding = 1;              // Texture array binding
182 |     write.dstArrayElement = slot;      // Which texture slot (0, 1, 2...)
183 |     write.descriptorType = VK_DESCRIPTOR_TYPE_COMBINED_IMAGE_SAMPLER;
184 |     write.descriptorCount = 1;
185 |     write.pImageInfo = &imageInfo;
186 |     
187 |     // Update BOTH double-buffered descriptor sets
188 |     write.dstSet = m_descriptorSet[0];
189 |     vkUpdateDescriptorSets(m_vkDevice, 1, &write, 0, nullptr);
190 |     
191 |     write.dstSet = m_descriptorSet[1];
192 |     vkUpdateDescriptorSets(m_vkDevice, 1, &write, 0, nullptr);
193 | }
194 | 
195 | void MegaGeometryRenderer::SetInstanceTexture(uint32_t instanceID, int32_t textureID) {
196 |     if (instanceID >= MAX_INSTANCES) return;
197 |     
198 |     // Update both CPU-mapped buffers (persistent mapping)
199 |     for (int i = 0; i < 2; ++i) {
200 |         if (m_instanceDataMapped[i]) {
201 |             m_instanceDataMapped[i][instanceID].textureID = (uint32_t)textureID;
202 |         }
203 |     }
204 | }
```

docs/reference/texture/more/VulkanDevice_Updated.cpp
```
1 | // ============================================================================
2 | // VULKAN DEVICE - UPDATED WITH DESCRIPTOR INDEXING SUPPORT
3 | // Critical for bindless texture arrays
4 | // ============================================================================
5 | 
6 | #ifndef WIN32_LEAN_AND_MEAN
7 | #define WIN32_LEAN_AND_MEAN
8 | #endif
9 | 
10 | #include "VulkanDevice.h"
11 | #include <vulkan/vulkan.h>
12 | 
13 | #if defined(SE_PLATFORM_WINDOWS)
14 |     #include <windows.h>
15 |     #include <vulkan/vulkan_win32.h>
16 | #elif defined(SE_PLATFORM_ANDROID)
17 |     #include <vulkan/vulkan_android.h>
18 | #endif
19 | 
20 | #include <cstdio>
21 | #include <vector>
22 | #include <SecretEngine/ILogger.h>
23 | 
24 | VulkanDevice::VulkanDevice(SecretEngine::ICore* core) : m_core(core) {}
25 | 
26 | VulkanDevice::~VulkanDevice() { 
27 |     Shutdown(); 
28 | }
29 | 
30 | bool VulkanDevice::Initialize() {
31 |     m_core->GetLogger()->LogInfo("VulkanDevice", "Starting Vulkan Initialization...");
32 | 
33 |     if (!CreateInstance()) {
34 |         m_core->GetLogger()->LogError("VulkanDevice", "CRITICAL: Failed to create Vulkan Instance.");
35 |         return false;
36 |     }
37 | 
38 |     if (!PickPhysicalDevice()) {
39 |         m_core->GetLogger()->LogError("VulkanDevice", "CRITICAL: Failed to find a suitable GPU.");
40 |         return false;
41 |     }
42 | 
43 |     if (!CreateLogicalDevice()) {
44 |         m_core->GetLogger()->LogError("VulkanDevice", "CRITICAL: Failed to create Logical Device.");
45 |         return false;
46 |     }
47 | 
48 |     m_core->GetLogger()->LogInfo("VulkanDevice", "Vulkan Hardware fully initialized.");
49 |     return true;
50 | }
51 | 
52 | bool VulkanDevice::CreateInstance() {
53 |     std::vector<const char*> extensions = { VK_KHR_SURFACE_EXTENSION_NAME };
54 | 
55 | #if defined(SE_PLATFORM_WINDOWS)
56 |     extensions.push_back(VK_KHR_WIN32_SURFACE_EXTENSION_NAME);
57 | #elif defined(SE_PLATFORM_ANDROID)
58 |     extensions.push_back(VK_KHR_ANDROID_SURFACE_EXTENSION_NAME);
59 | #endif
60 |     
61 |     VkApplicationInfo appInfo = { VK_STRUCTURE_TYPE_APPLICATION_INFO };
62 |     appInfo.pApplicationName = "SecretEngine";
63 |     appInfo.applicationVersion = VK_MAKE_VERSION(1, 0, 0);
64 |     appInfo.pEngineName = "SecretEngine";
65 |     appInfo.engineVersion = VK_MAKE_VERSION(1, 0, 0);
66 |     appInfo.apiVersion = VK_API_VERSION_1_2;  // Need 1.2 for descriptor indexing
67 | 
68 |     VkInstanceCreateInfo createInfo = { VK_STRUCTURE_TYPE_INSTANCE_CREATE_INFO };
69 |     createInfo.pApplicationInfo = &appInfo;
70 |     createInfo.enabledExtensionCount = static_cast<uint32_t>(extensions.size());
71 |     createInfo.ppEnabledExtensionNames = extensions.data();
72 | 
73 |     VkResult result = vkCreateInstance(&createInfo, nullptr, &m_instance);
74 |     if (result != VK_SUCCESS) {
75 |         char buf[128];
76 |         sprintf(buf, "vkCreateInstance failed with error code: %d", (int)result);
77 |         m_core->GetLogger()->LogError("VulkanDevice", buf);
78 |         return false;
79 |     }
80 | 
81 |     return true;
82 | }
83 | 
84 | bool VulkanDevice::CreateSurface(void* nativeWindow) {
85 |     VkResult result = VK_ERROR_INITIALIZATION_FAILED;
86 | 
87 |     if (!nativeWindow) {
88 |         m_core->GetLogger()->LogError("VulkanDevice", "Cannot create surface: Native Window handle is NULL.");
89 |         return false;
90 |     }
91 | 
92 | #if defined(SE_PLATFORM_WINDOWS)
93 |     VkWin32SurfaceCreateInfoKHR createInfo = { VK_STRUCTURE_TYPE_WIN32_SURFACE_CREATE_INFO_KHR };
94 |     createInfo.hwnd = (HWND)nativeWindow;
95 |     createInfo.hinstance = GetModuleHandle(nullptr);
96 |     result = vkCreateWin32SurfaceKHR(m_instance, &createInfo, nullptr, &m_surface);
97 | 
98 | #elif defined(SE_PLATFORM_ANDROID)
99 |     VkAndroidSurfaceCreateInfoKHR createInfo = { VK_STRUCTURE_TYPE_ANDROID_SURFACE_CREATE_INFO_KHR };
100 |     createInfo.pNext = nullptr;
101 |     createInfo.flags = 0;
102 |     createInfo.window = (ANativeWindow*)nativeWindow;
103 |     
104 |     result = vkCreateAndroidSurfaceKHR(m_instance, &createInfo, nullptr, &m_surface);
105 | #endif
106 | 
107 |     if (result != VK_SUCCESS) {
108 |         m_core->GetLogger()->LogError("VulkanDevice", "Failed to create Window Surface.");
109 |         return false;
110 |     }
111 |     return true;
112 | }
113 | 
114 | bool VulkanDevice::PickPhysicalDevice() {
115 |     uint32_t count = 0;
116 |     vkEnumeratePhysicalDevices(m_instance, &count, nullptr);
117 |     
118 |     if (count == 0) {
119 |         m_core->GetLogger()->LogError("VulkanDevice", "No Vulkan-compatible GPUs found on this system.");
120 |         return false;
121 |     }
122 | 
123 |     VkPhysicalDevice devices[8];
124 |     vkEnumeratePhysicalDevices(m_instance, &count, devices);
125 |     m_physicalDevice = devices[0]; 
126 | 
127 |     VkPhysicalDeviceProperties props;
128 |     vkGetPhysicalDeviceProperties(m_physicalDevice, &props);
129 |     m_core->GetLogger()->LogInfo("VulkanDevice", props.deviceName);
130 | 
131 |     return true;
132 | }
133 | 
134 | bool VulkanDevice::CreateLogicalDevice() {
135 |     // ============================================================================
136 |     // CRITICAL: Enable Descriptor Indexing Features for Bindless Textures
137 |     // ============================================================================
138 |     
139 |     // 1. Query descriptor indexing support
140 |     VkPhysicalDeviceDescriptorIndexingFeatures indexingFeatures = {
141 |         VK_STRUCTURE_TYPE_PHYSICAL_DEVICE_DESCRIPTOR_INDEXING_FEATURES
142 |     };
143 |     
144 |     VkPhysicalDeviceFeatures2 supportedFeatures = {
145 |         VK_STRUCTURE_TYPE_PHYSICAL_DEVICE_FEATURES_2
146 |     };
147 |     supportedFeatures.pNext = &indexingFeatures;
148 |     
149 |     vkGetPhysicalDeviceFeatures2(m_physicalDevice, &supportedFeatures);
150 |     
151 |     // 2. Log support status
152 |     if (indexingFeatures.shaderSampledImageArrayNonUniformIndexing) {
153 |         m_core->GetLogger()->LogInfo("VulkanDevice", "✓ Bindless textures supported (descriptor indexing)");
154 |     } else {
155 |         m_core->GetLogger()->LogWarning("VulkanDevice", "⚠ Bindless textures NOT supported on this GPU");
156 |     }
157 |     
158 |     // 3. Enable required features
159 |     VkPhysicalDeviceDescriptorIndexingFeatures enabledIndexingFeatures = {
160 |         VK_STRUCTURE_TYPE_PHYSICAL_DEVICE_DESCRIPTOR_INDEXING_FEATURES
161 |     };
162 |     enabledIndexingFeatures.shaderSampledImageArrayNonUniformIndexing = VK_TRUE;
163 |     enabledIndexingFeatures.runtimeDescriptorArray = VK_TRUE;
164 |     enabledIndexingFeatures.descriptorBindingPartiallyBound = VK_TRUE;
165 |     enabledIndexingFeatures.descriptorBindingVariableDescriptorCount = VK_TRUE;
166 |     enabledIndexingFeatures.descriptorBindingUpdateUnusedWhilePending = VK_TRUE;
167 |     
168 |     VkPhysicalDeviceFeatures2 deviceFeatures2 = {
169 |         VK_STRUCTURE_TYPE_PHYSICAL_DEVICE_FEATURES_2
170 |     };
171 |     deviceFeatures2.features.samplerAnisotropy = VK_TRUE;
172 |     deviceFeatures2.pNext = &enabledIndexingFeatures;
173 |     
174 |     // 4. Queue creation
175 |     float priority = 1.0f;
176 |     VkDeviceQueueCreateInfo queueInfo = { VK_STRUCTURE_TYPE_DEVICE_QUEUE_CREATE_INFO };
177 |     queueInfo.queueFamilyIndex = 0; 
178 |     queueInfo.queueCount = 1;
179 |     queueInfo.pQueuePriorities = &priority;
180 | 
181 |     // 5. Device extensions (MUST include descriptor indexing extension)
182 |     const char* deviceExtensions[] = { 
183 |         VK_KHR_SWAPCHAIN_EXTENSION_NAME,
184 |         VK_EXT_DESCRIPTOR_INDEXING_EXTENSION_NAME  // CRITICAL for bindless
185 |     };
186 |     
187 |     // 6. Device creation with features
188 |     VkDeviceCreateInfo createInfo = { VK_STRUCTURE_TYPE_DEVICE_CREATE_INFO };
189 |     createInfo.pNext = &deviceFeatures2;  // Chain the features
190 |     createInfo.queueCreateInfoCount = 1;
191 |     createInfo.pQueueCreateInfos = &queueInfo;
192 |     createInfo.enabledExtensionCount = 2;
193 |     createInfo.ppEnabledExtensionNames = deviceExtensions;
194 | 
195 |     VkResult result = vkCreateDevice(m_physicalDevice, &createInfo, nullptr, &m_device);
196 |     if (result != VK_SUCCESS) {
197 |         char buf[128];
198 |         sprintf(buf, "vkCreateDevice failed with error code: %d", (int)result);
199 |         m_core->GetLogger()->LogError("VulkanDevice", buf);
200 |         return false;
201 |     }
202 | 
203 |     vkGetDeviceQueue(m_device, 0, 0, &m_graphicsQueue);
204 |     return true;
205 | }
206 | 
207 | void VulkanDevice::Shutdown() {
208 |     if (m_surface) {
209 |         vkDestroySurfaceKHR(m_instance, m_surface, nullptr);
210 |         m_surface = VK_NULL_HANDLE;
211 |     }
212 |     if (m_device) {
213 |         vkDestroyDevice(m_device, nullptr);
214 |         m_device = VK_NULL_HANDLE;
215 |     }
216 |     if (m_instance) {
217 |         vkDestroyInstance(m_instance, nullptr);
218 |         m_instance = VK_NULL_HANDLE;
219 |     }
220 | }
```

2 | ␀GL_GOOGLE_cpp_style_line_directive␀␀␀␀GL_GOOGLE_include_directive␀␀␀␀␀␀main␀␀␀␀␀␀	␀␀␀localPos␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀inPosition␀␀␀␀␀␀␀worldPos␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀instRow0␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀instRow1␀␀␀␀␀␀$␀␀␀instRow2␀␀␀␀␀␀+␀␀␀gl_PerVertex␀␀␀␀␀␀+␀␀␀␀␀␀␀gl_Position␀␀␀+␀␀␀␀␀␀gl_PointSize␀␀␀␀␀␀+␀␀␀␀␀␀gl_ClipDistance␀␀␀+␀␀␀␀␀␀gl_CullDistance␀␀␀-␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀1␀␀␀PushConstants␀␀␀␀␀1␀␀␀␀␀␀␀viewProj␀␀␀␀␀␀3␀␀␀pc␀␀␀␀?␀␀␀N␀␀␀␀␀B␀␀␀inNormal␀␀␀␀␀␀R␀␀␀diff␀␀␀␀␀␀Y␀␀␀lighting␀␀␀␀␀␀_␀␀␀instanceColor␀␀␀␀␀a␀␀␀packedColor␀␀␀d␀␀␀outColor␀␀␀␀␀␀r␀␀␀inUV␀␀␀␀G␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀G␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀G␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀G␀␀$␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀G␀␀+␀␀␀␀␀␀H␀␀+␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀H␀␀+␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀H␀␀+␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀H␀␀+␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀G␀␀1␀␀␀␀␀␀H␀␀1␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀H␀␀1␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀H␀␀1␀␀␀␀␀␀␀#␀␀␀␀␀␀␀G␀␀B␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀G␀␀a␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀G␀␀d␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀G␀␀r␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀!␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀ ␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀ ␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀␀
